# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains reported benchmark design details, comparative outcome metrics for coding agents with and without context files, reported cost and token overhead, a quoted recommendation from the ETH Zurich paper, a contrasting efficiency study, and bounded practitioner guidance claims about what context files tend to include and omit.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The ETH Zurich study used AGENTbench with 138 software engineering tasks drawn from real GitHub pull requests across 12 Python repositories that already had developer-written context files.

## Source Excerpt

"The first was AGENTbench, a new benchmark of 138 software engineering tasks drawn from real GitHub pull requests across 12 Python repositories, each of which already contained a developer-written AGENTS.md or equivalent file."

## Note

This is a benchmark-construction detail reported by the source.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The ETH Zurich study also used SWE-bench Lite with 300 tasks as a comparison baseline.

## Source Excerpt

"The second was SWE-bench Lite, an existing benchmark of 300 tasks used as a comparison baseline."

## Note

This is a second evaluation-environment detail reported by the source.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study tested four coding agents under three conditions: no context file, an LLM-generated context file, and a developer-provided file.

## Source Excerpt

"They tested four coding agents across three conditions: no context file, an LLM-generated (large language model) context file, and the developer-provided file. The agents were Claude Code (Sonnet 4.5), Codex (GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.1 Mini), and Qwen Code (Qwen3-30B-Coder)."

## Note

The source names the model and condition set directly.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: result
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
LLM-generated context files reduced task success rates in 5 of 8 evaluation settings, with an average performance drop of 0.5 to 2 percentage points.

## Source Excerpt

"LLM-generated context files reduced task success rates in 5 of 8 evaluation settings, with an average performance drop of 0.5 to 2 percentage points."

## Note

This is a reported task-success outcome from the ETH Zurich study as described by the source.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: result
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Developer-provided context files showed approximately a 4 percentage point improvement on AGENTbench.

## Source Excerpt

"Developer-provided files fared better — showing approximately a 4 percentage point improvement on AGENTbench..."

## Note

The source reports this improvement specifically for AGENTbench.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents.md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
All context file types increased inference costs by 20 to 23%.

## Source Excerpt

"...all context file types, regardless of source, increased inference costs by 20 to 23%..."

## Note

This cost increase is reported as applying regardless of whether the file was LLM-generated or developer-provided.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Context files added an average of 2.45 to 3.92 additional steps per task.

## Source Excerpt

"...and added an average of 2.45 to 3.92 additional steps per task."

## Note

This is a separate overhead metric from cost or success rate.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For GPT-series models, reasoning tokens increased by 14 to 22% when a context file was present.

## Source Excerpt

"For GPT-series models, reasoning tokens increased by 14 to 22% when a context file was present."

## Note

The source reports this token overhead specifically for GPT-series models.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
When uv was mentioned in the context file, uv invocations increased by 1.6x.

## Source Excerpt

"The 1.6x increase in uv invocations when uv was mentioned in the context file illustrates the pattern — agents follow tooling instructions faithfully."

## Note

This is presented by the source as behavioral evidence that agents follow explicit tooling instructions.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors interpret context files primarily as execution constraints rather than navigation aids.

## Source Excerpt

"The authors' interpretation is that context files function primarily as execution constraints, not as navigation aids."

## Note

This is an interpretation reported by the source, not a directly measured outcome metric.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: quote
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The ETH Zurich paper recommends that human-written files describe "only minimal requirements to avoid making tasks unnecessarily difficult."

## Source Excerpt

"The researchers phrase their recommendation carefully: human-written files should describe 'only minimal requirements to avoid making tasks unnecessarily difficult.'"

## Note

This is a quoted recommendation rather than a benchmark result.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In the JAWs 2026 study, AGENTS.md was associated with a drop in median completion time from 98.57 to 70.34 seconds, a 28.64% reduction.

## Source Excerpt

"With AGENTS.md, median completion time dropped from 98.57 to 70.34 seconds — a 28.64% reduction."

## Note

This introduces tension with the ETH Zurich results because it reports an efficiency gain rather than a task-success outcome.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: metric
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In the JAWs 2026 study, AGENTS.md was associated with a 20% reduction in output tokens.

## Source Excerpt

"Output tokens fell by 20%. The results were statistically significant."

## Note

This token-efficiency result is reported from a different study design and sits in tension with ETH Zurich's reported success-rate degradation and cost increase.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Groves 2026, "When AGENTS.md Backfires"
source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
When existing documentation was removed from repositories, LLM-generated context files improved performance by 2.7%.

## Source Excerpt

"A revealing experiment in the paper removes existing documentation from repositories before testing. In that condition, LLM-generated context files improved performance by 2.7%."

## Note

This is a conditional result; the improvement is reported only after existing documentation was removed.
---

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains reported benchmark design details, comparative outcome metrics for coding agents with and without context files, reported cost and token overhead, a quoted recommendation from the ETH Zurich paper, a contrasting efficiency study, and bounded practitioner guidance claims about what context files tend to include and omit.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-008
evidence_id: evidence-01

source_url: https://notchrisgroves.com/when-agents-md-backfires/
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The ETH Zurich study used AGENTbench with 138 software engineering tasks drawn from real GitHub pull requests across 12 Python repositories that already had developer-written context files.

## Source Excerpt

"The first was AGENTbench, a new benchmark of 138 software engineering tasks drawn from real GitHub pull requests across 12 Python repositories, each of which already contained a developer-written AGENTS.md or equivalent file."

## Note

This is a benchmark-construction detail reported by the source.
```

The study tested four coding agents under three conditions: no context file, an LLM-generated context file, and a developer-provided file.

"They tested four coding agents across three conditions: no context file, an LLM-generated (large language model) context file, and the developer-provided file. The agents were Claude Code (Sonnet 4.5), Codex (GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.1 Mini), and Qwen Code (Qwen3-30B-Coder)."

## Note

The source names the model and condition set directly.
```

```
